# Data Model & Normalization Checklist: Canonical Filter Columns on `scraped_jobs`

**Purpose**: Validate the *quality* of the data-model and normalization requirements — vocabulary, precedence, per-site mapping, NULL/tri-state semantics, and the contract offered to the future consumer — before `/speckit-plan`.
**Created**: 2026-07-16
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command. It is a set of **unit tests for the requirements as written** — each item asks whether something is *specified* well enough to implement and verify, not whether code behaves. Complements [requirements.md](./requirements.md), which covers generic spec quality; this file goes after the data model specifically.

**Depth**: Pre-plan gate (author-facing). **Scope**: includes the forward contract to the not-yet-built filtering service, per FR-008c / FR-009c / FR-010a.

## Canonical Shape & Column Definition Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are value **bounds** specified for each of the five attributes (max length, permitted character set), or is bounding left entirely to the plan? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK002 Is a length bound specified for `education_requirements`, whose value may be an unbounded join of N labels **or** a long free-prose experience description? [Gap, Spec §FR-012, §FR-012b]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the expected form of `language` bounded (e.g. 2–3 characters after subtag removal), given the per-source column is `varchar(8)`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011a]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the spec state whether the two vocabulary attributes are constrained **at the data layer** (enumerated/checked) or only by projection behavior? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-004, §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK005 Are the five attributes' names free of collision with existing per-source column names — specifically, is the canonical `employment_type` distinguished in prose from Glassdoor's per-source column *also* named `employment_type`? [Clarity, Conflict, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK006 Is a requirement & acceptance-criteria ID scheme consistently applied across the new FR-005a–d / FR-008a–d / FR-009a–c / FR-010a–c / FR-011a–b / FR-012a–c sub-requirements? [Traceability]

## Vocabulary & Mapping Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is the normalized-token rule (trim, uppercase, spaces/hyphens → underscore) specified precisely enough to be applied identically to every source field? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005a]
- [ ] CHK008 Does the normalization rule define behavior for inputs it does not obviously cover — repeated separators, embedded punctuation, non-ASCII casing? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-005a]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the claim that the mapping tables are "exhaustive" consistent with FR-005b's admission that they are reasoned rather than observed? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005a, §FR-005b]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the **recognized-but-unmappable** list (`OTHER`) stated as closed, with an unambiguous rule for classifying any value not on it? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008d]
- [ ] CHK011 Are the six deliberately-unmapped tokens (`PERMANENT`, `FREELANCE`, `PER_DIEM`, `APPRENTICESHIP`, `COMMISSION`, `NEW_GRAD`) documented with the *reason* they are unmapped, so a later reader does not "helpfully" map them? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005c]
- [ ] CHK012 Is `language` distinguished from the two closed vocabularies — i.e. is it clear it validates **shape, not membership**, with no allow-list? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011b]
- [ ] CHK013 Is "a plausible language code" (FR-011b) quantified with objective criteria, or is "plausible" an unquantified adjective? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011b]
- [ ] CHK014 Is it specified whether the mapping tables are **normative requirements** or illustrative examples the plan may extend? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005a]

## Precedence & Selection Rules

- [ ] CHK015 Is the employment-type precedence rationale ("decreasing commitment") sufficient to justify every adjacent pair, e.g. why `CONTRACT` outranks `TEMPORARY`? [Measurability, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK016 Are precedence and the recognized/unrecognized/unmappable classification specified as an unambiguous *order of operations* (classify first, then rank), rather than two rules that could be applied in either order? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008b, §FR-008d]
- [ ] CHK017 For Glassdoor's structured-vs-header employment fallback, is **"absent"** defined — does an empty list trigger the fallback, or only a NULL field? [Ambiguity, Gap, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK018 Is the "structured field wins, header ignored entirely" rule stated unambiguously enough that a header value can never leak in when the structured field is present-but-unmappable (e.g. `OTHER`)? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-005, §FR-008d]
- [ ] CHK019 Is determinism specified as a property of the *rules* (order-independence) rather than only asserted as a test outcome? [Measurability, Spec §FR-008, §SC-003a]

## NULL & Tri-State Semantics

- [ ] CHK020 Is NULL's meaning stated **per column**, given FR-002 assigns it one global meaning ("this site did not say")? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK021 **Is the overloading of NULL addressed?** FR-002 defines NULL as "the site did not say", but FR-023 gives pre-existing rows NULL for the same columns — making "not yet populated" and "site said nothing" indistinguishable to any consumer. [Conflict, Spec §FR-002, §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK022 Is the tri-state `salary_disclosed` requirement free of contradiction between "NULL MUST NOT be collapsed into false" (FR-003) and the per-site mapping table's NULL column? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, §FR-005a]
- [ ] CHK023 Are the conditions that produce NULL *without* a warning enumerated distinctly from those that produce NULL *with* one? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013, §FR-014, §FR-008d]
- [ ] CHK024 Is it specified whether an all-unrecognized value list is distinguishable in the data from "the site said nothing", and is accepting that indistinguishability recorded as a decision? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Cross-Column Consistency

- [ ] CHK025 **Do `salary_disclosed` and the shipped salary amounts describe the same figures?** *Verified against the live builder*: Glassdoor's `salary_source` comes from `jobDetailsData.salarySource` (`jobs.py:611`) while `jsonld_salary_min`/`max` come from the employer-authored JSON-LD `baseSalary` block (`jobs.py:628`) — **two different payloads**. A row could therefore carry employer-sourced amounts flagged as a site estimate, or the reverse. Is this addressed? [Conflict, Gap, Spec §FR-010, §FR-005a]
- [ ] CHK025a Is it acknowledged that shipped 008 already has this split — Glassdoor's canonical `salary_period` also derives from `jobDetailsData` (`jobs.py:610`) while its amounts derive from JSON-LD — so `salary_disclosed` **inherits** an existing cross-payload inconsistency rather than introducing one? [Assumption, Spec §FR-020, §Context]
- [ ] CHK026 Is it specified whether `salary_disclosed` may be true/false when `salary_min` and `salary_max` are both NULL — i.e. a disclosure claim about figures that do not exist? [Gap, Spec §FR-010a]
- [ ] CHK027 Are the `workplace_type` ↔ `remote` divergences specified for **all three sites**, or only the two called out (Glassdoor FR-009a, LinkedIn FR-009b)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009a, §FR-009b, §FR-009c]
- [ ] CHK028 Is `education_requirements`' duplication of `experience_level` accompanied by any requirement letting a consumer tell the two apart when they hold identical text? [Gap, Spec §FR-012a]
- [ ] CHK029 Are the five new attributes' relationships to the existing 22 columns documented in one place, or must a reader assemble them from scattered requirements? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020]

## Consumer Contract (Forward-Looking)

- [ ] CHK030 Are the consumer obligations (FR-008c "follow `source_row_id` back", FR-009c "MUST NOT mix them within a single filter", FR-010a positive-evidence semantics) stated anywhere a consumer is actually *bound* by them, or only in prose the consumer's authors may never read? [Gap, Spec §FR-008c, §FR-009c]
- [ ] CHK031 Is FR-009c's "a consumer MUST choose one column and not mix them" backed by any enforcement or discoverability mechanism, or is it advisory only? [Measurability, Spec §FR-009c]
- [ ] CHK032 Is the FR-009a obligation to record the `remote` discrepancy in `docs/live-per-source-schemas.md` reflected in any Success Criterion, or can the feature be declared done without it? [Gap, Traceability, Spec §FR-009a, §SC-001–009]
- [ ] CHK033 Is the caveat that Indeed's `ONSITE` means only "not remote" (FR-009) captured anywhere durable, given it is the one value the canonical row asserts beyond what the site said? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK034 Are the three accepted-cost decisions stated with enough precision that a future reader can recognize when the cost has become unacceptable and reverse them? [Clarity, Spec §Decisions]

## Observability & Self-Correction

- [ ] CHK035 Is a **stable, greppable warning identifier** specified for each new warning class (mirroring the shipped `projection_unknown_salary_period` / `projection_bad_posted_at` convention)? FR-005d's required review depends on finding these. [Gap, Spec §FR-005d, §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK036 Is the warning payload specified (which fields, truncation limits) consistently with existing projection warnings? [Consistency, Gap, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK037 Is FR-005d's "first scan" defined well enough to be verifiable — specifically, what if that scan returns no postings from a given site, leaving that site's mapping unverified? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-005d, §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK038 Is the FR-009b contradiction warning distinguished from a vocabulary-drift warning, so the FR-005d review is not polluted by upstream data problems that need no mapping change? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009b, §FR-005d]
- [ ] CHK039 Is SC-009's "zero warnings for normal postings" objectively checkable given the mapping is admittedly unverified — i.e. can it be evaluated *before* FR-005d's review? [Measurability, Conflict, Spec §SC-009, §FR-005b]

## Scenario & Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK040 Are requirements defined for the re-scrape/conflict path — whether an existing canonical row's five attributes are recomputed or left stale on re-ingest? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK041 Are exception-flow requirements defined for a source value of an unexpected *type* (e.g. a jsonb object where a list is expected), distinct from an unexpected *token*? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK042 Is FR-019 ("no posting ingested today may fail") strong enough to cover every new mapping path, given each added transform is a new failure surface? [Coverage, Spec §FR-019]

## Acceptance Criteria & Constitution Obligations

- [ ] CHK043 **Does the spec require a new smoke test covering the five attributes?** Constitution Principle II requires permanent new behavior to be captured by one; FR-022 only requires *existing* tests to keep passing. [Gap, Conflict, Spec §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK044 Is the existing `smoke_test_scraped_jobs_merge.py` (the 008 projection test, and the closest analogue) identified anywhere in the spec as the test most likely to need extension? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK044a **Is FR-022's "all existing smoke tests" resolvable to an accurate list?** *Verified*: four `smoke_test_*.py` files exist, but Constitution Principle II enumerates only three — it omits `smoke_test_scraped_jobs_merge.py`, added by feature 008. FR-022 inherits the stale list by reference. [Conflict, Gap, Spec §FR-022, Constitution §II]
- [ ] CHK045 Is SC-007 ("ingest throughput materially unchanged") measurable, or is "materially" unquantified? [Measurability, Ambiguity, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK046 Is the Assumptions claim that the table self-populates "within one shelf-life" verifiable without the shelf-life duration being stated anywhere? [Assumption, Gap, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK047 Are the migration requirements (new columns chained off head `030`, no new indexes) stated as requirements, or only implied by the Assumptions section? [Traceability, Gap, Spec §Assumptions]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference

**Highest-signal items** (these look like genuine defects in the requirements, not just polish). Two were verified against the code and are stated as fact; the rest are gaps in what the spec says:

- **CHK025 / CHK025a** — *verified*: Glassdoor's `salary_source` and its canonical salary amounts come from **two different payloads** (`jobDetailsData` vs JSON-LD), so `salary_disclosed` may describe figures other than the row's own `salary_min`/`max`. Shipped 008 already splits this way for `salary_period`, so this is inherited, not introduced — but `salary_disclosed` is the column whose whole purpose is provenance, which makes it the one place the split actually bites.
- **CHK044a** — *verified*: four smoke tests exist; Constitution §II lists three. FR-022 says "all existing smoke tests" and inherits the stale list.
- **CHK021** — NULL is overloaded: "site didn't say" (FR-002) and "row predates the feature" (FR-023) are the same value, indistinguishable to any consumer.
- **CHK043** — no requirement for *new* smoke-test coverage, which Constitution §II appears to mandate for permanent new behavior.
- **CHK035** — FR-005d's required warning review specifies no warning identifier to review by.
- **CHK032** — the FR-009a documentation obligation is bound by no Success Criterion, so the feature can be "done" without it.

**Suggested triage**: CHK025/CHK025a and CHK021 are semantic and worth resolving in the spec before planning. CHK043/CHK044a and CHK032/CHK035 are additive requirements the plan can absorb if named now.
